page: Microsoft Azure E4_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the Ev5 server family. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor with an x86_64 architecture, this instance provides 4 dedicated vCPUs mapped from 2 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The CPU operates at a clock speed of 2.8 GHz and features a 48 MB L3 cache. It is provisioned with 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high resource density of 8.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. This configuration does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, and it is deployed via the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor.

Benchmark results indicate moderate single-core performance alongside weaker multi-core scaling. The single-core stress-ng and Geekbench tests place the processor in the average tier, whereas multi-core CPU benchmarks, including Passmark and multi-threaded Geekbench, fall into the bottom 25%. Memory performance is mixed; while the Passmark memory score is average, large-block read and write memory bandwidth benchmarks are positioned in the weak category. Database operations, Redis SET throughput, and static web serving benchmarks also register in the lower 25% tier.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the E4_v5 offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio, making it a viable option for memory-intensive workloads that do not require high-performance compute scaling or local storage. Given the 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and the benchmark profile, this server is suited for relational databases with moderate transaction volumes, caching layers, and memory-heavy enterprise applications where memory capacity is prioritized over raw multi-threaded processing power.
description: Microsoft Azure E4_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the Ev5 family, featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs and 32.0 GB of RAM. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor running at 2.8 GHz on the x86_64 architecture, this instance delivers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs. Performance benchmarks show average single-core capabilities, though multi-core compute, memory bandwidth, and database operations fall into the lower 25% tier. This resource profile makes the E4_v5 a qualitatively cost-effective choice for memory-bound workloads that do not demand intensive parallel processing. It is best suited for moderate database hosting, caching systems, and memory-intensive enterprise applications.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring a high RAM-to-vCPU ratio for memory-intensive workloads requiring moderate single-core processing power.
